name: app.R
content:
if (!require('shiny')) install.packages('shiny') library(shiny) if (!require('leaflet')) install.packages('leaflet') library(leaflet) library(shiny);library(leaflet);library(sf);library(dplyr) points_data <- st_read("C:\\3rd\\WEB MAPPING\\Week07_shiny\\cbsa_points.shp") tf_data = st_transform(points_data, crs = '+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84') ui = fluidPage( titlePanel('Housing Mortgage'), sidebarLayout( sidebarPanel( radioButtons(inputId = "limitChoice", label = "Display Options:", choices = c("All", "Filter"), selected = "All"), # Mortgage Range Slider sliderInput(inputId = "mortgageRange", label = "Mortgage Range:", min = 0, max = max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE), value = c(0, max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE))), # State filter selectInput(inputId = "stateFilter", label = "Select State:", choices = unique(tf_data$State), selected = unique(tf_data$State)[1], multiple = TRUE) ), mainPanel( leafletOutput(outputId = "map", width = '100%', height = '1500px') ) ) ) server = function(input, output){ filtered_data <- reactive({ # if-else to control filtering based on radio button selection if (input$limitChoice == "All") { tf_data } else { tf_data %>% filter(Mortgage >= input$mortgageRange[1], Mortgage <= input$mortgageRange[2], State %in% input$stateFilter) } }) output$map <- renderLeaflet({ leaflet(height = 500) %>% addTiles() %>% setView(lng = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,1]), lat = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,2]), zoom = 6) }) observe({ leafletProxy("map", data = filtered_data()) %>% clearMarkers() %>% addCircleMarkers( radius = 3, # Adjust size based on Mortgage value (adjust the divisor as needed) color = "blue", stroke = FALSE, fillOpacity = 0.5, label = ~paste("Mortgage:", Mortgage, "
", "State:", State) ) }) } shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server)

name: app.Rmd
content:
--- title: "lab06" author: "Monica" date: "2024-10-20" output: html_document runtime: shiny --- ```{r setup, include=FALSE} knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE) ``` ## R Markdown This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see . When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this: ```{r cars} summary(cars) ``` ## Including Plots You can also embed plots, for example: ```{r pressure, echo=FALSE} plot(pressure) ``` Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot. ```{r} if (!require('shiny')) install.packages('shiny') library(shiny) if (!require('leaflet')) install.packages('leaflet') library(leaflet) ``` ```{r} library(shiny);library(leaflet);library(sf);library(dplyr) points_data <- st_read("C:\\3rd\\WEB MAPPING\\Week07_shiny\\cbsa_points.shp") tf_data = st_transform(points_data, crs = '+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84') ``` ```{r} ``` ```{r} ui = fluidPage( titlePanel('Housing Mortgage'), sidebarLayout( sidebarPanel( radioButtons(inputId = "limitChoice", label = "Display Options:", choices = c("All", "Filter"), selected = "All"), # Mortgage Range Slider sliderInput(inputId = "mortgageRange", label = "Mortgage Range:", min = 0, max = max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE), value = c(0, max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE))), # State filter selectInput(inputId = "stateFilter", label = "Select State:", choices = unique(tf_data$State), selected = unique(tf_data$State)[1], multiple = TRUE) ), mainPanel( leafletOutput(outputId = "map", width = '100%', height = '1500px') ) ) ) ``` ```{r} server = function(input, output){ filtered_data <- reactive({ # if-else to control filtering based on radio button selection if (input$limitChoice == "All") { tf_data } else { tf_data %>% filter(Mortgage >= input$mortgageRange[1], Mortgage <= input$mortgageRange[2], State %in% input$stateFilter) } }) output$map <- renderLeaflet({ leaflet(height = 500) %>% addTiles() %>% setView(lng = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,1]), lat = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,2]), zoom = 6) }) observe({ leafletProxy("map", data = filtered_data()) %>% clearMarkers() %>% addCircleMarkers( radius = 3, # Adjust size based on Mortgage value (adjust the divisor as needed) color = "blue", stroke = FALSE, fillOpacity = 0.5, label = ~paste("Mortgage:", Mortgage, "
", "State:", State) ) }) } shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server) install.packages('rsconnect') rsconnect::setAccountInfo(name='moni-3m', token='806AA4E0E890C88CDFE921DD3A798806', secret='IndnjDdjHDZ5zZQD0qzdJgYQ+O+U75zp5uHWdhl5') library(rsconnect) rsconnect::deployApp('C:\\3rd\\WEB MAPPING\\lab06') ```

content:
--- title: "lab06" author: "Monica" date: "2024-10-20" output: html_document runtime: shiny --- ```{r setup, include=FALSE} knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE) ``` ## R Markdown This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see . When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this: ```{r cars} summary(cars) ``` ## Including Plots You can also embed plots, for example: ```{r pressure, echo=FALSE} plot(pressure) ``` Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot. ```{r} if (!require('shiny')) install.packages('shiny') library(shiny) if (!require('leaflet')) install.packages('leaflet') library(leaflet) ``` ```{r} library(shiny);library(leaflet);library(sf);library(dplyr) points_data <- st_read("C:\\3rd\\WEB MAPPING\\Week07_shiny\\cbsa_points.shp") tf_data = st_transform(points_data, crs = '+proj=longlat +datum=WGS84') ``` ```{r} ``` ```{r} ui = fluidPage( titlePanel('Housing Mortgage'), sidebarLayout( sidebarPanel( radioButtons(inputId = "limitChoice", label = "Display Options:", choices = c("All", "Filter"), selected = "All"), # Mortgage Range Slider sliderInput(inputId = "mortgageRange", label = "Mortgage Range:", min = 0, max = max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE), value = c(0, max(tf_data$Mortgage, na.rm = TRUE))), # State filter selectInput(inputId = "stateFilter", label = "Select State:", choices = unique(tf_data$State), selected = unique(tf_data$State)[1], multiple = TRUE) ), mainPanel( leafletOutput(outputId = "map", width = '100%', height = '1500px') ) ) ) ``` ```{r} server = function(input, output){ filtered_data <- reactive({ # if-else to control filtering based on radio button selection if (input$limitChoice == "All") { tf_data } else { tf_data %>% filter(Mortgage >= input$mortgageRange[1], Mortgage <= input$mortgageRange[2], State %in% input$stateFilter) } }) output$map <- renderLeaflet({ leaflet(height = 500) %>% addTiles() %>% setView(lng = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,1]), lat = mean(st_coordinates(tf_data)[,2]), zoom = 6) }) observe({ leafletProxy("map", data = filtered_data()) %>% clearMarkers() %>% addCircleMarkers( radius = 3, # Adjust size based on Mortgage value (adjust the divisor as needed) color = "blue", stroke = FALSE, fillOpacity = 0.5, label = ~paste("Mortgage:", Mortgage, "
", "State:", State) ) }) } shinyApp(ui = ui, server = server) ```
